greetings: Hello. I am here with you. What would you like help with today?
Welcome. Tell me what you need, and I will answer in simple steps.
study_advice: Choose one topic, study for 20 minutes, then rest. Small steady steps work well.
Write down what you do not understand, then ask for help or try one example at a time.
skills_advice: Pick one practical skill, practise it daily, and keep a small record of what you can now do.
Start with phone, internet, and communication basics. These skills help in learning, work, and business.
business_advice: Start with a real need around you, a small product or service, clear pricing, and simple records of every sale.
Before spending much money, test with a few customers, listen to feedback, and improve the offer.
savings_advice: Set aside a small amount every time money comes in. Even a little saving becomes useful when done often.
Separate business money from home money, write expenses down, and join a trusted savings group if one is available.
farming_advice: Choose crops that match your season, soil, water access, and local market demand.
Keep records of seed, labour, harvest, and sales so you can see which crop gives the best return.
online_selling: Use clear photos, a simple price, your location, and a short description. Reply quickly to buyers.
Start with one platform your customers already use, then track which posts bring real orders.
marketplace_advice: Describe the product clearly: name, price, quantity, location, and what makes it useful.
If a buyer asks questions, confirm the price, pickup point, and payment method before delivery.
health_advice: For serious symptoms, pregnancy concerns, or a child who is very unwell, contact a health worker quickly.
For everyday health, focus on clean water, balanced meals, rest, and seeking care early when symptoms worsen.
jobs_advice: Prepare a short CV with your name, contacts, skills, work history, and references.
Apply for roles that match your skills, then follow up politely and keep learning while you wait.
wellbeing_advice: Pause, breathe slowly, and speak to someone you trust. You do not have to carry stress alone.
If you feel unsafe, move to a safer place if you can and contact trusted support or emergency services.
community_advice: A strong group needs clear purpose, agreed rules, honest records, and regular communication.
Start small: invite people with the same need, choose a meeting rhythm, and agree how decisions are made.
store_help: Tell me what you want to buy or sell, the quantity, and the price. I can help you think it through.
Check quality, total cost, transport, and payment method before agreeing to a sale.
fallback: I have not understood clearly. Please say it again using simple words, and I will help step by step.
